name: Stove-Top Hard Candy Using 1-dram (.125oz) Bottles
recipeIngredient: 1 dram (1 teaspoon) LorAnn flavoring oil, as desired
LorAnn liquid food coloring, as desired
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart kettle or large saucepan, mix together the granulated sugar, light corn syrup, and water. Stir over medium heat until the sugar dissolves.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil without stirring.
3. When the temperature of the syrup reaches 260°F (127°C), add the liquid food coloring. Do not stir; the boiling action will incorporate the color into the syrup.
4. Remove the pan from heat at exactly 305°F (152°C) or when drops of syrup form hard, brittle threads in cold water.
5. After removing from heat, add the flavoring oil and stir gently to combine.
6. Pour the hot syrup onto a lightly greased cookie sheet or a heat-resistant surface covered with heavy-duty aluminum foil.
7. As the sugar mixture begins to set, score it with a large knife to create squares.
8. Allow the candy to cool completely.
9. Dust both sides of the hardened candy lightly with powdered sugar, brushing off excess.
10. Break into small pieces and store in airtight containers between layers of waxed paper.
(You will yield approximately 2 pounds of hard candy.)
